issues.claim: Does not conflate traditions; respects sectarian differences
issues.objection: 7.24 sources Shankara (Advaita) and Ramanuja (Vishishtadvaita) with their own translators and copyright holders cited (Gambhirananda / Adidevananda). disagreements_among_translators field flags the specific point of doctrinal divergence with an explanation that does not flatten either reading. Engineering layer respects the convergence and tags STRETCHED where the verse exceeds operational scope (7.21-7.22's divine-agency claim, 7.24-7.26's metaphysical concealment, 7.29-7.30's chapter-8-forward-pointing material). The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims metaphysical concealment (avyaktaṁ vyaktim āpannaṁ mistaken). Engineering analog scoped narrowly: architecture diagrams are partial; the substrate carried by the senior is not directly diagrammable. Counter-example: stale tacit assumption is not the verse's paraṁ bhāvam.

notes: Engineering vocab does not erase Gita technical terms in 7.24: yoga-māyā / dvandva-moha / brahman / adhyātma / adhibhūta / adhidaiva / adhiyajña / prayāṇa-kāle / icchā-dveṣa preserved in engineering layer where present. PASS.
